specific_mechanism: MFG-LLM: Mean-Field-Game policy fine-tuning for multi-LLM-agent populations. Represent the agent population by an empirical state distribution m_t in a low-dim embedding space. Compute discretized MFG equilibrium policy π*(x; m) via fictitious play / proximal-point updates on the coupled HJB-FP system. Each LLM-agent π_i is fine-tuned (via DPO or PPO) against the equilibrium reference π*.
llm_application: MFG-LLM: maintain a 32-dim embedding state per LLM-agent (mean output sentiment, expected length, role-vector). Empirical distribution m_t over N agents drives a continuum cost L(x_i, action, m). Solve discretized MFG by alternating: (1) HJB: optimize each agent's value given current m, (2) FP: update m_t+1 by simulating actions of best-response policies. Use π*(x; m) as fine-tuning target for each agent.
novelty_claim: Bayesian Nash Equilibrium for LLM-MAS exists (arXiv 2506.08292 ICML 2025); Game-theoretic LLM lens exists (arXiv 2601.15047). MFG-LLM is specifically the N→∞ continuum limit, less explored.
motivation_justification: MFG provides the rigorous PDE framework for N→∞ multi-agent games. The candidate transfers the HJB-FP coupled equilibrium to LLM-agent populations.
sub_mechanisms: M_4: Per-agent fine-tuning toward equilibrium policy π*
content_words: mean field game theory hjb fokker planck
fictitious play proximal point coupled pde
DPO PPO reference policy per-agent target
